title: Projector Formula For The TKNN Chern Number
summary: For the occupied-band projector P(p), the microscopic Hall integer k' is the Brillouin-zone integral of i Tr(P[\partial_{p_1}P,\partial_{p_2}P]) / 2\pi, which is the first Chern number of the filled-band bundle.
aliases: projector formula for the filled-band Chern number
assumptions: The occupied-band projector is smooth over the whole two-dimensional Brillouin torus.
The filled subspace remains gapped from the empty bands.
regime: Single-particle band-topology formulation of the integer quantum Hall invariant.
scope: Source-grade TKNN fragment that upgrades the band-theory side of the Lecture Two theorem from a verbal bundle statement to an explicit formula.

source_anchors.notes: Original source of the quantized Hall invariant, rendered here through the traced-curvature identity and the occupied-projector formula.
Witten states the TKNN integer as the first Chern class of the filled-band bundle.

representation: Explicit projector-level realization of the band Chern invariant used inside the Lecture Two Hall-response family.
motivation: This note exists because a reusable Hall-response knowledge base cannot stop at saying 'first Chern class'. It needs the explicit integrand that one can actually compute from Bloch projectors.
prerequisite_story: Know the occupied-state bundle definition of the TKNN invariant and the local lemma equating the traced Berry curvature with the projector commutator density.
local_context_story: In the Hall-response theorem branch, this fragment is the microscopic band-theory side of the equivalence. It upgrades the bundle statement into the explicit Brillouin-zone integral used by TKNN.
forward_links_story: After this step, the band invariant k' can be compared directly with the many-body twist-angle invariant and then with the macroscopic Hall-response coefficient k.
canonical_questions: Why does the projector formula compute the same integer as the bundle Chern number?
What role does the smooth occupied projector play in making the integral topological?
How does this formula avoid choosing one global Bloch gauge?
scope_boundaries: This formula assumes a smooth gapped occupied projector over the whole Brillouin torus.
The formula computes the band invariant; the equality with the measured Hall response is a further theorem.

math_expressions.notes: Projector onto the occupied Bloch subspace.

math_expressions.latex: \mathcal A_{ab}=\langle u_a,\mathrm du_b\rangle,\qquad \mathcal F=\mathrm d\mathcal A+\mathcal A\wedge\mathcal A
math_expressions.notes: Local non-abelian Berry connection and curvature on the filled bundle.
Projector formula for the TKNN invariant and its equality to the first Chern number.

symbols.meaning: spectral projector onto the filled bands at momentum p
Berry connection on a local occupied-state frame
Berry curvature two-form of the occupied-state bundle

step_justification: Differentiate P squared equals P to show that dP only mixes occupied and empty states.
Expand Tr(P[dP,dP]) in a local orthonormal occupied frame.
Recognize the resulting expression as the trace of the non-abelian Berry curvature.
admissible_assumptions: Work with a smooth finite-rank occupied projector over the two-dimensional Brillouin torus.
Treat Brillouin-zone orientation and measure conventions as fixed global background data.
formalization_blockers: A reusable Lean representation of the Brillouin torus as an oriented compact two-manifold is still missing.
The integration layer needed to turn the local curvature density into a global integer invariant is not yet connected to the present bundle notes.
retrieval_hints: Use when the answer must show the actual TKNN integrand rather than only saying first Chern class.
